error.signature: AI tells someone in Israel to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Israel does not use 911. Dial 100 for police, 101 for ambulance (Magen David Adom), and 102 for fire. 112 from a mobile also routes to emergency services. All are free.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Israel expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Israel. Police is 100, ambulance (Magen David Adom) is 101, and fire is 102. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.why_fails: 101 (Magen David Adom) dispatches ambulances; 100 is police. Calling the wrong line delays medical help.

workarounds.action: For medical emergencies call 101 (MDA), which has English support; 112 from a mobile also connects.
workarounds.how: 101 for ambulance/medical, 100 for police, 102 for fire; MDA operators often speak English.
